reviews.review_description:
Can only agree with all the other 5-star reviews, and hope it will stay non-subscription so as many people as possible can enjoy this game in the future.

Full version allows perfect adjustment of desired intensity level, as well as use of own mp3's up to 15 mins long.

The trailer for the game is really bad though and urgently needs an update 😂

I am an old, fat, mostly sedentary lady. I started out playing Beat Saber In Oculus because that's what everyone else played. Don't get me wrong It's fun. But I don't feel like it's a workout unless you're using a very fast song at a very high level. As it older lady I don't know too many of the new songs and artists so they really don't interest me. I'm also not into really fast dance music. Fitness fills the spot for me. I like that you can a just adjust it to make it work better for you. I like that music is kind of dance related but not so difficult that I can't start out there. I very much like that there are different types of movements. Also at only 20 bucks instead of 20 bucks a month for supernatural, it really is perfect for me. It may not be a workout for healthier people but it is for me. While my budget does not allow me to purchase it at the moment It's going into the budget next month and I look forward to exploring the app more fully at that point.

Beat Saber meets Fitness Training. The demo offers enough to make your mind about it (except a tutorial, or I didn't see it. I think it would be good). More squats than other games of the genre so it's a plus. Music is okay and the concept is awesome. Demo features 180° range of motion and various intensities. You'll definitely sweat and have fun for a while just with the free version. Will definitely buy.

reviews.review_title: Lots of fun. Bit convoluted for fitness.
reviews.review_description:
Superb game, however I think it tries to hard and over complicates things. Putting skulls on targets so if you hit them you lose is so frustrating and unnecessary, especially if you get into a flow sometimes you cannot see them till it's too late.

You change from hitting, to slicing, to defending with shields. Good for a challenge game but breaks the flow for fitness.

It may just take practice. Personally I would have only stuck to the punching and slicing for this game and remove the shields and skulls. 

Very nice graphics though!

I loved the app it caters well for me as I have health issues and can alter the settings to fit what is comfortable for me. Not all of us want to be top of the leader board and outdo everyone else, but to still get a full workout without being scrutinised. My only issue is I can't buy the full version why is this?

developer_response.body:
Full version of X-Fitness is available in the store already.
Thank you for your patience.

reviews.review_description: I appreciate this title, all in all I'm satisfied with the demo, bearing in mind that I haven't been drawn to fitness games, but I liked this one for the style, polish and playability. I discovered just now that the developers are the same as the x-fighters I bought and that I really like, keep it up!
Great game and challenges. Too bad no cross buy. Already, ownd PC version not the Quest 2.

I'm another one who's come over to the absolutely excellent X-BOOSTER from FitXR after that was destroyed by the recent updates.

Having read through some of the reviews here I've seen pretty much everything has been covered but perhaps it's not been made clear enough that you can remove all of the extras and just get a really excellent flowing boxercise routine easily setup.

I popped the MP3s for my favourite Drum and Bass album onto my Quest in the "Music" folder via SideQuest. Then in X-BOOSTER I went to Program and under that I edited the Alpha program and added all my tracks (after very quickly composing them the way I wanted them).

Then I switched off the elevator and the skulls in the options.

Now I have an excellent 45 minute workout that I can do on a daily basis.

The fact that it's working out to music that I love will keep me coming back as I always felt forced when using the rubbish music that you usually get on these kinds of apps.

I can create various different workouts/programs all using my favourite music.

Amazing!

This game needs a Discord and masses of promotion on Reddit/YouTube etc right now as we need to get the word out on how good it is.

It's what BOXVR could and should have been.

The addition of object density control would make it absolute heaven as someone else has mentioned.

reviews.review_title: GOOD but need to buy again?
reviews.review_description: This is my favorite game on Steam (where I bought it), but I would like to play it without a cable, that means to pay $20 more.. :-/

...for another certain boxing related game.

However, I found in the demo that many of my hits (especially for slicing spheres) were not registered. I don't know if it's because I missed or because hit detection failed. I expect a combination of both.

I like that Skulls can be turned off, but could we also turn off the obstacles? I have a small play space and cannot dodge them. I also cannot squat so easily due to age. At the very least will the full game have an option to disable squatting obstacles?

If the full game is regular updated to fix bugs, etc., and could add in some more accessibility options, I'll buy this as soon as I can next month.

I do like this.  But for me needs 1 thing. Far different item placement. So you have punching, slicing, and blocking. 
1-when I was at the slicing part I was looking for far more intricate placement of the items you slice. That part you practically want to feel like you just pulled off some smooth intricate ninja moves. 
2-change up the blaster shots that come at you. An example: arms straight out , right arm raised 12 inches , left arm lowered 12 inches to hit the first shots. Then change up and reverse arms for second shot. Do that 2 times . Then bring both arms in to chest to catch the next shots. Next push  hands away to the right and left to catch the next 8 shots . 4 shots each side . Then finish with pulling hands to chest held up for a 2 handed block. 
This particular beat would be da-da-da-da(bababababa)boom(block)

Sorry if I am not explaining well. Also I tried demo and finished the 3 difficulties rather easy and I’m a 50 year old out of shape guy. 
So,I’m looking for far more intricate patterns to punch slice and block. You want to finish the workout feeling (wow that was amazing)
This has huge potential though and worth a go at it.

reviews.review_title: Needs a lot of work before I'd buy it
reviews.review_description:
The devs of this app should have been all over the fallout of FitXR and doing everything possible to capture all the refugees looking for a new home, but my experience with the demo was not appealing.

Colors are very harsh on the eyes, too much "stuff" is thrown at you too quickly in the workouts and the settings do not allow for granular control to customize the elements that you want in your workout. The colorful, flashy environment is just too busy. If it's updated in the future to allow user customization, I'll revisit it.

reviews.review_title: Thank you for the wonderful application.
reviews.review_description:
This demo version was so good that it's one of the applications I'm considering buying the full version.

There are many good points, but there are four dissatisfaction points.

1.I want a mode that reduces notes and earns points with punch quality.
2.It would be nice if the hit sound was a little more refreshing.
3.Visualization of added points on hits and addition of effects on critical hits.
4.The display shifts when the mode is set to Japanese.

I think there are some difficulties, but I would be grateful if you could consider it.
I'm using a translation site, so I'm sorry if the text is strange.
I really liked it but now when I'm experiencing trouble with the app I find no way for contact.

reviews.review_title: Very enjoyable - Fun and Intense Workout
reviews.review_description: Well designed game. Nicely done!

Having been burned from purchasing FitXr - now subscription only, this is one of the top contenders to take its place.
Top BIG dev recommendations:
1. Allow us to turn off obstacles. Preferably have choices for those you duck/squat and those to sidestep - independent on/off toggles.
2. Allow setting the height of the squat obstacles, independent from player height which affects all the punching heights.
3. Absolutely improve the 3-dimensionality of the punch-bags - something FitXr got right.
4. Avoid having objects to be shielded so low they're literally near ankle height.
5. Allow choice of glove colours - pallette or RGB picker and to swap.
Great potential in this expanding app in a very much growing VR market!
Ps. If and when just #1 obstacle toggle is addressed that will seal my purchase - currently the only deal-breaker.
EDIT: From reviews, apparently #1 & swapping #5 is done but not in the demo??! If so, you're losing customers by not showing that or even indicating those are there when you upgrade.

reviews.review_title: Great app a must have on fitness
reviews.review_description: It is a great fitness al to Burn calories.a lot of different workouts  most are around 2:30 minuten. There short and fast pace. Love to see in next update to see workouts added of 5 to 10 minuten.

I was hooked right off the bat with the different things to do in each song...plus I LOVE the music...( I could do  away with the red balls though..( not enough room to deal with them)...but other than that..it’s definitely worth buying the full version....I’ve played it lol day.kudos to the team who made this

reviews.review_title: Better than FitXR but with some flaws
reviews.review_description: I use FitXR and tried Supernatural and I prefer X-Booster for a couple reasons. 1) The variety of moves. In addition to the standard boxing moves you also have the sword and shield play of Beat Saber. 2) You can work out with your own songs and they work great with perfect timing to the moves. However, I am experiencing two issues: 1) Some of my moves are not registered even though I swing in time. 2) Sometimes when the targets came at me from the side, they are outside my guardian and I an unable to hit them without risking literally hitting a wall. I am on the fence about upgrading to the paid version. If they are able to fix these issues, or if they don't bother you, I would recommend this over FitXR, Supernatural, and Beat Saber.

developer_response.body:
Hi, Thanks for your opinion.
We are improving the game all the time so your comment is important for us. 
If you don't have too much space in the room and you're afraid of hitting the wall, in SETTINGS you can turn on a SMALL ROOM option.

reviews.review_description: Good calorie burner and quite immersive.
